title: R code used in the publiction "Efficient secretion of a plastic degrading enzyme in Chlamydomonas reinhardtii"
author: Molino, João Vitor
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Code Descriptions for Dataset Metadata 
 
 
  production_curve.R  
 
  Description:  This R script generates and analyzes production curves for enzyme activity over time. It includes data processing, normalization, and visualization of enzyme production in a microbial system. 
  Inputs:  Raw enzyme production data in .csv or .txt format. 
  Outputs:  Plots and tables summarizing the enzyme production curves. 
  File Path:   /path/to/data/production_curve.R  
 
 
 
  production_curve_LLM.R  
 
  Description:  This R script builds upon the production curve analysis but applies machine learning models (likely linear models or least-squares regressions) to enhance prediction and interpretation of enzyme production trends. 
  Inputs:  Enzyme production data in .csv format, with options for model selection. 
  Outputs:  Predictive model plots and performance metrics. 
  File Path:   /path/to/data/production_curve_LLM.R  
 
 
 
  Spectrophotometer_calibration_curve.R  
 
  Description:  This script is designed for creating calibration curves for spectrophotometric assays. It reads absorbance data and fits a model to standard concentrations, providing calibration curves for quantification. 
  Inputs:  Calibration standard data from spectrophotometer readings. 
  Outputs:  Calibration curve plots and coefficients for further quantifications. 
  File Path:   /path/to/data/Spectrophotometer_calibration_curve.R  
 
 
 
  TPA 240nm.R  
 
  Description:  This R script processes and analyzes data from a TPA (Terephthalic Acid) degradation assay, particularly at 240 nm absorbance. The script generates plots and quantitative measures of TPA breakdown. 
  Inputs:  TPA assay data. 
  Outputs:  Plots of TPA degradation over time at 240 nm. 
  File Path:   /path/to/data/TPA_240nm.R  
 
 
 
  FDA_Screening_plot_v2.R  
 
  Description:  This script processes and visualizes screening data for FDA assays, likely evaluating enzyme activity or other biochemical markers. The script is optimized for high-throughput screening data. 
  Inputs:  FDA assay results in .csv format. 
  Outputs:  Screening plots highlighting key results and statistical summaries. 
  File Path:   /path/to/data/FDA_Screening_plot_v2.R  
 
 
 
  MS_data_v2.R  
 
  Description:  This script handles mass spectrometry (MS) data, processing it to extract relevant peak information, normalize samples, and compare different conditions. It focuses on spectral data analysis and peak identification. 
  Inputs:  MS data files (e.g., .mzML, .csv). 
  Outputs:  Processed MS spectra, peak tables, and comparative plots. 
  File Path:   /path/to/data/MS_data_v2.R  
 
 
 
  Impra_Spectra.R  
 
  Description:  This R script processes and analyzes spectroscopic data related to Impranil degradation. It reads absorbance data from multiple samples and generates comparative spectra plots. 
  Inputs:  Impranil degradation spectroscopic data. 
  Outputs:  Spectra plots comparing degradation rates and key metrics. 
  File Path:   /path/to/data/Impra_Spectra.R  
 
 
 
  Impranil_Screening_plotv2.R  
 
  Description:  This script visualizes the results from an Impranil degradation screening experiment. It generates plots that highlight enzyme activity or degradation rates across different experimental conditions. 
  Inputs:  Impranil degradation screening data. 
  Outputs:  Comparative screening plots with statistical analyses. 
  File Path:   /path/to/data/Impranil_Screening_plotv2.R
